title: Across ERs, Tylenol orders for pregnant people dropped after health officials linked drug to autism
summary: Immediately after U.S. health officials linked Tylenol to autism, the drug's use in pregnant women visiting ERs dropped, new study shows.

title: STAT+: Omada reports first profitable quarter as it seizes broad GLP-1 opportunity
summary: Virtual chronic care company Omada, which went public less than a year ago, reported a quarterly profit for the first time.

title: STAT+: Science Corp. raises $230 million to bring retinal implant to Americans
summary: Science Corp. raised $230 million as it awaits a decision from the FDA on PRIMA, its wireless retinal implant.

title: STAT+: Patient health data as a public utility: A former ARPA-H data chief explains
summary: Patients generate vast health data, but private companies monetize it. A policy group proposes treating the data like a regulated utility.

title: FDA Grants Third Approval Under the National Priority Voucher Program
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved teclistamab in combination with daratumumab hyaluronidase-fihj, Tec-Dara, to treat adult patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who…

title: Breaching the IBD efficacy ceiling, and sham surgeries
summary: Spyre Therapeutics CEO Cameron Turtle talks about the search for an IBD treatment on this week's "The Readout LOUD."

title: STAT+: TrumpRx’s slow start
summary: A month in, we check in on President Trump's drug discount website.

title: Lilly targets employers in new bid to broaden access to obesity drugs
summary: A service tailored to people with workplace-based insurance coverage represents a new way for Lilly to bypass insurers and expand use of its popular obesity shot.

title: STAT+: A drug company with a side of gold?
summary: GLP-1s and addiction, a gene therapy company and a mining corporation, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: PepGen muscular dystrophy drug gets ‘surprise’ hold from FDA
summary: The agency acted after reviewing mouse data submitted in 2024, making the timing of the new pause “confusing,” one analyst wrote.

title: Pediatric nicotine exposure shifts with trends
summary: A new mystery about Jeffrey Epstein's Harvard tissue sample, TrumpRx limitations, and other health news

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about TrumpRx shortcomings, Lilly targeting employers, and more
summary: A month after its launch, few drugs are available on TrumpRx and data about how much the site is being used remain unknown

title: FDA to Address Unused Opioids in American Homes
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a Request for Information (RFI) seeking public comment on potential new standards for in-home opioid disposal products.

title: A startup making drugs for blood disorders banks $160M
summary: Atavistik Bio is using the cash to develop “allosteric” medicines it believes can precisely impact on diseases like myelofibrosis and the blood condition HHT.

title: STAT+: The extremism of the FDA’s Marks and Prasad has come with costs
summary: With the current leadership of the FDA, the rare-disease community is suffering whiplash. Drugmakers are frustrated. And investors are sitting on their wallets.

title: STAT+: More than 50 medical schools will expand nutrition education in agreement with RFK Jr.
summary: At a time when his vaccine policies are causing pushback from doctors, Kennedy's allies push nutrition education as an area of common ground.

title: STAT+: Eli Lilly launches program for employers to subsidize cost of obesity drug outside insurance
summary: Eli Lilly is going beyond its efforts to sell its obesity drug Zepbound directly to patients, and is now trying to target employers.

title: STAT+: Cognito raises $105 million for Alzheimer’s treatment device
summary: Cognito raised $105 million as it awaits trial data that it hopes will convince the FDA to authorize its Alzheimer’s treatment device.

title: A month in, TrumpRx falls short of president’s grand promises
summary: Trump wants to focus on his efforts to lower drug prices, but the TrumpRx website is falling short of his promises.

title: Opinion: AI could revolutionize antibiotics — but the market is standing in the way
summary: Economic reality and the persistent absence of funding stand in the way of using AI for antibiotic breakthroughs.

title: STAT+: Gilead and South Africa are negotiating a license for local production of new HIV drug
summary: South Africa has the world’s highest HIV burden, with about 8 million HIV-positive people, or 1 in 5 adults, as of last year.

title: STAT+: A new mystery emerges about Epstein’s involvement in Harvard genetics study
summary: Someone seems to have altered the Personal Genome Project’s profile page believed to belong to Epstein to indicate he provided consent to join the study Jan. 31, 2026.

title: Opinion: Obesity drugs may silence the ‘drug noise’ behind all addiction
summary: A new study found GLP-1 drugs were associated with 50% fewer substance-related deaths, 39% fewer drug overdoses, and 26% fewer drug-related hospitalizations.

title: STAT+: RFK Jr. has wide discretion to choose evidence to support vaccine decisions, DOJ argues
summary: A Justice Department lawyer argued that the HHS secretary has broad discretion to issue vaccine guidance and choose what evidence to consider.

title: Moderna to pay up to $2.25B to end mRNA vaccine patent fight
summary: Announced days before a trial, the deal settles a longstanding dispute and avoids what one analyst called a “worst-case scenario” financially for Moderna.

title: Blackstone puts $400M into Teva, Sanofi gut disease drug
summary: The deal adds to a growing biotech presence for Blackstone and supports testing of a drug competing with rival therapies from Merck and Roche.

title: STAT+: Is RFK Jr. coming for your Dunkin’?
summary: The warning comes as Kennedy is leaning heavily into his agenda to improve American diets

title: Prime to test FDA flexibility with 2-patient gene editing submission
summary: The planned filing comes amid heightened scrutiny of the FDA’s rare disease stance and a year after Prime deprioritized the program for economic reasons.

title: FDA again targets GLP-1 compounders; Intellia to restart heart disease trial
summary: The agency zeroed in on claims that compounded GLP-1 drugs are comparable to approved medicines. Elsewhere, a blood pressure drug topped Wall Street expectations and a Merck deal paid dividends.

title: Digital endpoints in clinical trials: building the business case for systematic adoption
FDA Warns 30 Telehealth Companies Against Illegal Marketing of Compounded GLP-1s
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today announced the issuance of 30 warning letters to telehealth companies for making false or misleading claims regarding compounded GLP-1 products offered on…

title: Kyowa Kirin abandons touted eczema drug following safety review
summary: A month after Amgen gave up on rocatinlimab — which was once seen as a threat to Dupixent — new findings have linked the drug’s mechanism to the onset of a type of skin cancer.

title: Pierre Fabre seeks to revive US approval chances for spurned cell therapy
summary: Ebvallo is one of a series of rare disease treatments recently turned back by the FDA amid shifting guidance that has surprised some companies and frustrated investors.

title: Author Correction: Novel drug treatments for schizophrenia
Promoting remyelination
Targeting vascular smooth muscle cells
An allosteric site on cereblon
Inhibiting advillin in glioblastoma
Ascendis wins FDA approval of dwarfism drug
summary: Yuviwel, a once-weekly injection, will now compete for market share with BioMarin’s lucrative daily shot Voxzogo.
